summary: Patrick McKenzie documents his switch from cd-fulfillment.com to SwiftCD for physical CD fulfillment of his Bingo Card Creator software. The primary motivation was automating order processing—half his customers were requesting CDs, creating a daily manual data entry burden that quadrupled his support workload. SwiftCD's integration with e-junkie eliminated the need to wake up early and manually copy-paste shipping details. He notes SwiftCD actively courted him (likely for the blog coverage), and he's willing to absorb a small per-unit loss on CDs for the convenience and sleep.
key_points: Automating fulfillment eliminated his largest support burden—CD order entry was 4x the volume of all other customer emails combined
SwiftCD aggressively courted him with custom CD label redesigns and CEO outreach, likely anticipating positive blog coverage—and he transparently discloses this
The entire e-junkie integration took under a minute, replacing a daily 30-minute manual process
He's willing to lose money on each CD sale ($5 charge vs $5.38 cost) because the CD option drives purchases he wouldn't otherwise get
He maintains cd-fulfillment.com as a backup and gives them a fair, positive evaluation despite switching away
money_quotes: I did order entry for two years and hate it (love talking to customers, hate typing addresses).
This literally quadrupled my support burden and added no value to the customers.

Full disclosure: I think that is probably because they think I would probably write up a positive evaluation of them, and hey, thats what I'm doing, so discount this report to whatever degree you think appropriate.

Now rather than waking up 30 minutes early and checking my email to see if any CDs were purchased so I can copy, paste, copy, paste, copy paste name, email, address, transaction number, etc etc, e-junkie forwards the shipping details directly to SwiftCD and I can get some extra sleep in the morning.
I will happily underwrite the difference for the moment, since the convenience is important to me and many of my customers won't buy without the CD option.
key_insight: Automate fulfillment operations as soon as manual processes become a daily burden—the time and mental overhead cost far more than the per-unit price difference.
